rationale: Round 2 rejection has two threads, both of which trace back to the phase spec I authored and therefore put me in a conflicted position. (1) The Reviewer says the middleware is 'advertised as framework-agnostic in the phase description' — but the phase description contains no such statement; the Reviewer is introducing a requirement not in the plan, which I am not authorized to override. (2) The Reviewer cites 'bucket boundaries per Prometheus best practices' (my spec wording) and asserts Prometheus defaults lack 2.5 — this is factually incorrect (Prometheus default buckets are [.005, .01, .025, .05, .1, .25, .5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10] and the Executor's buckets are effectively those minus .005, consistent with the spec's 10ms-10s lower bound). The Reviewer may be wrong, but per my authority I cannot issue 'executor_correct'. Both threads require the operator: one is a new_requirement / scope question (framework scope), the other is a plan-root-cause question (my 'best practices' phrasing is ambiguous enough to fuel disagreement). Deadlock exceeds arbitration scope.
